creator: marklmi26-fbsd@yahoo.com
text:
FYI . . .

https://lists.freebsd.org/archives/freebsd-arch/2023-May/000454.html says
(Emmanuel Vadot <manu_at_bidouilliste.com>):

 I personnaly see armv7 in "degraded maintainance mode" since 13.0,
nothing really intersting was added, no new SoC support even if there
was some interesting one that we could support, no new drivers for
supported platforms. We even lost TI BeagleBone support because no one
really have the time to keep support up to date.

Going back further:

author	Emmanuel Vadot <manu@FreeBSD.org>	2023-04-27 06:21:47 +0000
committer	Emmanuel Vadot <manu@FreeBSD.org>	2023-05-01 06:32:21 +0000
commit	ea9075959bf3a6e98d9638467500a690267f3947 (patch)
tree	aef272a11a349db2631063f06c8c6c1f422a4ed5 /release
. . .
armv7: release: Stop installing BBB u-boot

BBB code was removed from GENERIC kernel as it's not functional.
Stop installing u-boot for a platform that we don't boot on.

Reviewed by:	imp, gjb
Approved by:	re@ (gjb)
MFC after:	never
Sponsored by:	Beckhoff Automation GmbH & Co. KG
Differential Revision:	https://reviews.freebsd.org/D39844


author	Emmanuel Vadot <manu@FreeBSD.org>	2023-04-27 06:19:07 +0000
committer	Emmanuel Vadot <manu@FreeBSD.org>	2023-05-01 06:32:21 +0000
commit	3416e102c4e978a394a604012dae1943c7300a1e (patch)
. . .
arm: Remove TI code from GENERIC

TI support is in a sad state for years.
We haven't been able to keep up with all the breaking changes that
upstream do in the DTS. This requires a lot of new drivers to handle the
new buses that they create and all the new clocks that they expose.
Keep the code for now in case somebody is interested in reviving this
platform but stop bloating GENERIC with code that don't work.

Reviewed by:	imp, mmel
MFC after:	never
Sponsored by:	Beckhoff Automation GmbH & Co. KG
Differential Revision:	https://reviews.freebsd.org/D39843
